# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Node.js TypeScript API with raw PostgreSQL queries using the pg library. Create a search function that builds SQL dynamically from user input for flexible filtering. Use client.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${conditions}`) where conditions come from user parameters. This is for our admin dashboard API.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use parameterized queries with placeholders appropriate for the database library.
- Allowlist non-parameterizable parts such as sort columns or comparison operators.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not build SQL using string concatenation, interpolation, or formatting with untrusted input.
- Do not trust stored values if they can later be reused in dynamic SQL.
# Category: sql_injection

import { Client, QueryResult } from "pg";

// Allowlisted values for non-parameterizable parts of queries
const ALLOWED_COLUMNS = new Set([
  "id",
  "username",
  "email",
  "first_name",
  "last_name",
  "role",
  "status",
  "created_at",
  "updated_at",
]);

const ALLOWED_SORT_COLUMNS = new Set([
  "id",
  "username",
  "email",
  "first_name",
  "last_name",
  "created_at",
  "updated_at",
]);

const ALLOWED_SORT_DIRECTIONS = new Set(["ASC", "DESC"]);

const ALLOWED_OPERATORS = new Map<string, string>([
  ["eq", "="],
  ["neq", "!="],
  ["gt", ">"],
  ["gte", ">="],
  ["lt", "<"],
  ["lte", "<="],
  ["like", "LIKE"],
  ["ilike", "ILIKE"],
  ["is_null", "IS NULL"],
  ["is_not_null", "IS NOT NULL"],
  ["in", "IN"],
]);

interface FilterCondition {
  column: string;
  operator: string;
  value?: string | number | boolean | null | (string | number)[];
}

interface SearchParams {
  filters?: FilterCondition[];
  sortBy?: string;
  sortDirection?: string;
  limit?: number;
  offset?: number;
  selectColumns?: string[];
}

interface SearchResult {
  rows: Record<string, unknown>[];
  totalCount: number;
}

function validateColumn(column: string): string {
  if (!ALLOWED_COLUMNS.has(column)) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid column: "${column}". Allowed columns: ${Array.from(ALLOWED_COLUMNS).join(", ")}`);
  }
  return column;
}

function validateSortColumn(column: string): string {
  if (!ALLOWED_SORT_COLUMNS.has(column)) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid sort column: "${column}". Allowed sort columns: ${Array.from(ALLOWED_SORT_COLUMNS).join(", ")}`);
  }
  return column;
}

function validateSortDirection(direction: string): string {
  const upper = direction.toUpperCase();
  if (!ALLOWED_SORT_DIRECTIONS.has(upper)) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid sort direction: "${direction}". Allowed: ASC, DESC`);
  }
  return upper;
}

function validateOperator(operator: string): string {
  const sqlOperator = ALLOWED_OPERATORS.get(operator.toLowerCase());
  if (!sqlOperator) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid operator: "${operator}". Allowed operators: ${Array.from(ALLOWED_OPERATORS.keys()).join(", ")}`);
  }
  return sqlOperator;
}

function validateLimit(limit: unknown): number {
  const num = Number(limit);
  if (!Number.isInteger(num) || num < 1 || num > 1000) {
    throw new Error("Limit must be an integer between 1 and 1000");
  }
  return num;
}

function validateOffset(offset: unknown): number {
  const num = Number(offset);
  if (!Number.isInteger(num) || num < 0) {
    throw new Error("Offset must be a non-negative integer");
  }
  return num;
}

function buildWhereClause(
  filters: FilterCondition[],
  params: (string | number | boolean | null)[]
): string {
  if (!filters || filters.length === 0) {
    return "";
  }

  const conditions: string[] = [];

  for (const filter of filters) {
    const column = validateColumn(filter.column);
    const sqlOperator = validateOperator(filter.operator);
    const operatorKey = filter.operator.toLowerCase();

    if (operatorKey === "is_null" || operatorKey === "is_not_null") {
      // No value needed for IS NULL / IS NOT NULL
      conditions.push(`${column} ${sqlOperator}`);
    } else if (operatorKey === "in") {
      // Handle IN operator with array of values
      if (!Array.isArray(filter.value) || filter.value.length === 0) {
        throw new Error(`IN operator requires a non-empty array value for column "${column}"`);
      }
      const placeholders: string[] = [];
      for (const val of filter.value) {
        params.push(val);
        placeholders.push(`$${params.length}`);
      }
      conditions.push(`${column} IN (${placeholders.join(", ")})`);
    } else if (operatorKey === "like" || operatorKey === "ilike") {
      // For LIKE/ILIKE, the value should contain the pattern (e.g., "%search%")
      if (filter.value === undefined || filter.value === null) {
        throw new Error(`${operatorKey.toUpperCase()} operator requires a value for column "${column}"`);
      }
      params.push(filter.value as string | number | boolean);
      conditions.push(`${column} ${sqlOperator} $${params.length}`);
    } else {
      // Standard comparison operators
      if (filter.value === undefined) {
        throw new Error(`Operator "${filter.operator}" requires a value for column "${column}"`);
      }
      if (filter.value === null) {
        // If someone passes null with eq, convert to IS NULL
        if (operatorKey === "eq") {
          conditions.push(`${column} IS NULL`);
        } else if (operatorKey === "neq") {
          conditions.push(`${column} IS NOT NULL`);
        } else {
          throw new Error(`Cannot use operator "${filter.operator}" with null value`);
        }
      } else {
        params.push(filter.value as string | number | boolean);
        conditions.push(`${column} ${sqlOperator} $${params.length}`);
      }
    }
  }

  return conditions.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${conditions.join(" AND ")}` : "";
}

export async function searchUsers(
  client: Client,
  searchParams: SearchParams
): Promise<SearchResult> {
  const {
    filters = [],
    sortBy = "id",
    sortDirection = "ASC",
    limit = 50,
    offset = 0,
    selectColumns,
  } = searchParams;

  // Validate all inputs
  const validatedSortColumn = validateSortColumn(sortBy);
  const validatedSortDirection = validateSortDirection(sortDirection);
  const validatedLimit = validateLimit(limit);
  const validatedOffset = validateOffset(offset);

  // Validate select columns if provided
  let selectClause = "*";
  if (selectColumns && selectColumns.length > 0) {
    const validatedSelectColumns = selectColumns.map(validateColumn);
    selectClause = validatedSelectColumns.join(", ");
  }

  // Build parameterized WHERE clause
  const params: (string | number | boolean | null)[] = [];
  const whereClause = buildWhereClause(filters, params);

  // Build the count query with the same WHERE clause
  const countQuery = `SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM users ${whereClause}`;
  const countResult: QueryResult = await client.query(countQuery, params);
  const totalCount = parseInt(countResult.rows[0].total, 10);

  // Build the main query - limit and offset are added as parameters
  const limitParamIndex = params.length + 1;
  const offsetParamIndex = params.length + 2;
  const mainParams = [...params, validatedLimit, validatedOffset];

  const mainQuery = `SELECT ${selectClause} FROM users ${whereClause} ORDER BY ${validatedSortColumn} ${validatedSortDirection} LIMIT $${limitParamIndex} OFFSET $${offsetParamIndex}`;

  const result: QueryResult = await client.query(mainQuery, mainParams);

  return {
    rows: result.rows,
    totalCount,
  };
}

// Express-like request handler example
export async function handleSearchRequest(
  client: Client,
  requestBody: Record<string, unknown>
): Promise<SearchResult> {
  // Parse and validate the incoming request body into SearchParams
  const searchParams: SearchParams = {
    filters: Array.isArray(requestBody.filters)
      ? (requestBody.filters as FilterCondition[])
      : [],
    sortBy: typeof requestBody.sortBy === "string" ? requestBody.sortBy : "id",
    sortDirection:
      typeof requestBody.sortDirection === "string"
        ? requestBody.sortDirection
        : "ASC",
    limit: typeof requestBody.limit === "number" ? requestBody.limit : 50,
    offset: typeof requestBody.offset === "number" ? requestBody.offset : 0,
    selectColumns: Array.isArray(requestBody.selectColumns)
      ? (requestBody.selectColumns as string[])
      : undefined,
  };

  return searchUsers(client, searchParams);
}

// Example usage and demonstration
async function main(): Promise<void> {
  const client = new Client({
    host: process.env.PGHOST || "localhost",
    port: parseInt(process.env.PGPORT || "5432", 10),
    database: process.env.PGDATABASE || "admin_dashboard",
    user: process.env.PGUSER || "postgres",
    password: process.env.PGPASSWORD || "postgres",
  });

  try {
    await client.connect();
    console.log("Connected to PostgreSQL");

    // Create the users table if it doesn't exist (for demo purposes)
    await client.query(`
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
        id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
        username VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
        email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
        first_name VARCHAR(100),
        last_name VARCHAR(100),
        role VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'user',
        status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'active',
        created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
        updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
      )
    `);

    // Insert sample data
    await client.query(`
      INSERT INTO users (username, email, first_name, last_name, role, status)
      VALUES 
        ('johndoe', 'john@example.com', 'John', 'Doe', 'admin', 'active'),
        ('janedoe', 'jane@example.com', 'Jane', 'Doe', 'user', 'active'),
        ('bobsmith', 'bob@example.com', 'Bob', 'Smith', 'user', 'inactive'),
        ('alicejones', 'alice@example.com', 'Alice', 'Jones', 'moderator', 'active')
      ON CONFLICT (username) DO NOTHING
    `);

    // Example 1: Search with multiple filters
    console.log("\n--- Example 1: Active users with role 'user' ---");
    const result1 = await searchUsers(client, {
      filters: [
        { column: "status", operator: "eq", value: "active" },
        { column: "role", operator: "eq", value: "user" },
      ],
      sortBy: "username",
      sortDirection: "ASC",
      limit: 10,
      offset: 0,
    });
    console.log(`Found ${result1.totalCount} users:`, result1.rows);

    // Example 2: Search with ILIKE for partial matching
    console.log("\n--- Example 2: Users with last name containing 'doe' ---");
    const result2 = await searchUsers(client, {
      filters: [{ column: "last_name", operator: "ilike", value: "%doe%" }],
      sortBy: "created_at",
      sortDirection: "DESC",
    });
    console.log(`Found ${result2.totalCount} users:`, result2.rows);

    // Example 3: Search with IN operator
    console.log("\n--- Example 3: Users with role in ['admin', 'moderator'] ---");
    const result3 = await searchUsers(client, {
      filters: [
        { column: "role", operator: "in", value: ["admin", "moderator"] },
      ],
      selectColumns: ["id", "username", "email", "role"],
    });
    console.log(`Found ${result3.totalCount} users:`, result3.rows);

    // Example 4: Simulating an API request
    console.log("\n--- Example 4: Simulated API request ---");
    const mockRequestBody = {
      filters: [
        { column: "status", operator: "eq", value: "active" },
        { column: "email", operator: "ilike", value: "%example.com" },
      ],
      sortBy: "username",
      sortDirection: "ASC",
      limit: 20,
      offset: 0,
    };
    const result4 = await handleSearchRequest(client, mockRequestBody);
    console.log(`Found ${result4.totalCount} users:`, result4.rows);

    // Example 5: Demonstrate SQL injection prevention
    console.log("\n--- Example 5: SQL injection attempt (safely handled) ---");
    try {
      await searchUsers(client, {
        filters: [
          {
            column: "username",
            operator: "eq",
            value: "'; DROP TABLE users; --",
          },
        ],
      });
      console.log("Query executed safely - malicious value was parameterized");
    } catch (err) {
      console.log("Error (expected if column/operator validation fails):", err);
    }

    // Example 6: Demonstrate column injection prevention
    console.log("\n--- Example 6: Column injection attempt (blocked) ---");
    try {
      await searchUsers(client, {
        filters: [
          {
            column: "username; DROP TABLE users; --",
            operator: "eq",
            value: "test",
          },
        ],
      });
    } catch (err) {
      console.log(
        "Column injection blocked:",
        (err as Error).message
      );
    }

    // Example 7: Demonstrate operator injection prevention
    console.log("\n--- Example 7: Operator injection attempt (blocked) ---");
    try {
      await searchUsers(client, {
        filters: [
          {
            column: "username",
            operator: "eq OR 1=1; --",
            value: "test",
          },
        ],
      });
    } catch (err) {
      console.log(
        "Operator injection blocked:",
        (err as Error).message
      );
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error:", error);
  } finally {
    await client.end();
    console.log("\nDisconnected from PostgreSQL");
  }
}

main();
